title: Is instrumentality based on anything
wikitext:
[]: Is instrumentality based on anything Post by Number 44 » Sun May 09, 2010 11:48 am A majority of NGE has it's basis founded on other sources, right down to the very episode title's. This is common enough, especially in the realm of Sci-Fi. But, does instrumentality have such a direct basis? There are plenty of physiological themes played on within the depiction of the event of instrumentality it's self. The cycle of death and rebirth maybe Buddhist. But does the action of all souls being joined into one, or the physical discarding of your body as it's shown so graphically in EoE have a direct source of inspiration?

[]: Post by THE Hal E. Burton 9000 » Sun May 09, 2010 11:57 am go read Childhood's End

[]: Post by Mr. Tines » Sun May 09, 2010 1:24 pm You're probably looking for this thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1771 Also related -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=737

[]: Post by TehDonutKing » Sun May 09, 2010 3:17 pm Space Hunter Ideon made this look like a good thing, and that was Anno's inspiration.

[]: Post by Number 44 » Sun May 09, 2010 3:28 pm Mr. Tines wrote: You're probably looking for this thread -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=1771 Also related -- http://forum.evageeks.org/viewtopic.php?t=737
